# Test fixtures

## sagemath_bmals.json — agreement fixture (generated, committed)

Generated by `scripts/export_sagemath_fixture.py` from the user's BM-ALS paper
repo (`D:\Projects\Hypermatrix ML`, `stage1/data/reviewer/multi_seed_n*/`).
10 cases: the n=4 (ell=3) and n=8 (ell=4) interaction tensors estimated from
dynamical-systems trajectories, 5 seeds each, with the SageMath
greedy-deflation BM-ALS final relative Frobenius error per case.

Format:

    {
      "cases": [
        {
          "name": "n8_seed42_ell4",
          "ell": 4,
          "tensor": [[[ ... ]]],
          "sage_rel_error": 0.00835,
          "convention_check_rel_error": 0.00835
        }
      ]
    }

`tensor` axes are (i, j, k) with bmx stacking on mode 3. The exporter
self-validates conventions: it rebuilds the SageMath factors in bmx layout,
reconstructs with bmx's `bmp`, and refuses to write if the error doesn't
reproduce `sage_rel_error` (`convention_check_rel_error` records the check).

`tests/test_sagemath_agreement.py` asserts bmx RE <= 1.1 * sage RE + 1e-9 —
we may do better (as of 2026-06 RALS beats the SageMath solver by 3–10 orders
of magnitude on every case); we must not do meaningfully worse.

## reviewer_results.json — reference data (not a test fixture)

Aggregate results from the BM-ALS paper's reviewer experiments (per-method
errors across ranks/seeds, timings, advantage ratios). No tensors inside, so
it cannot drive the agreement test; kept as the reference distribution for
what the paper's solver achieved on its benchmarks.
